title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Eye Swelling
intro: Herbal teas for eye swelling, also known as periorbital edema, are natural remedies used to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort around the eyes. These teas are effective in treating this ailment due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing. Examples of herbal teas used to treat eye swelling include chamomile, which soothes and calms the skin, green tea, which contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation, and peppermint, which has a cooling effect that helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling. Other herbal teas, such as calendula, lavender, and ginger, are also used to treat eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to promote healing and reduce discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling ailments, such as conjunctivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing swelling and redness in the eyes, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, play a crucial role in modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation in the eyes. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can benefit from the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, leading to faster recovery and reduced risk of complications associated with eye swelling.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea if desired for taste.
Drink the tea warm 2-3 times a day as needed for eye swelling relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat eye swelling, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. Precautions when using this herbal preparation include avoiding consumption if you have any allergies or sensitivities, and being cautious of potential interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea for eye swelling.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, with 8-12 hours interval between each cup.
Start with small doses (1 teaspoon) and gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if needed.
Continue using the tea for 5-7 days to see noticeable improvement in eye swelling.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the eye swelling ailment known as periorbital edema. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and prevent infection, making it an effective remedy for this condition. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis, including triterpene saponins and carotenoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promote wound healing, which helps to alleviate eye swelling. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis teas can provide relief from eye swelling and promote a faster recovery, making it a valuable herbal remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2 teaspoons of dried Calendula officinalis flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Steep the Calendula flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink the tea warm, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce eye swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Calendula officinalis teas for eye swelling may be effective due to ethnobotanical and pharmacological studies suggesting that this medicinal plant is effective for ophthalmological problems.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat eye swelling ailments, but it may cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, redness, and itching, in some individuals. When using Calendula officinalis teas to treat eye swelling, it is essential to take precautions, such as applying a small patch test on the skin before consumption, and avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of Calendula officinalis in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and wring it out.
Apply the tea-soaked cloth to the affected eye for 5-10 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat for 3-5 days or until the swelling subsides, using 1 tablespoon per application.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat the eye swelling ailment, also known as edema, due to its anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The anti-inflammatory effects of Aloe barbadensis teas can be attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-ecdysone, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. By reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of excess fluids from the affected area, Aloe barbadensis teas help to alleviate the symptoms of edema and promote healing. The benefits of using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat eye swelling include reduced swelling, pain relief, and improved overall eye health, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Cut a 3-inch piece of Aloe barbadensis leaf. Remove the outer skin and chop the inner gel into small pieces.
In a cup, boil 1 cup of water. Let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of chopped Aloe gel to the cooled water. Stir well.
Let the mixture sit for 10-15 minutes to allow the Aloe gel to infuse. Strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the Aloe tea slowly. Repeat as needed for eye swelling relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat eye swelling, but it may cause mild side effects such as skin irritation, itching, or burning sensation due to its aloe vera gel's potency and potential allergic reactions. To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to start with a small amount, avoid applying it near the eyes for an extended period, and wash your hands thoroughly after handling the tea to prevent any accidental contact with the eyes.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in cooled Aloe barbadensis tea
Place the cloth over the swollen eye for 10 minutes
Repeat the process 2-3 times a day for best results
Use 1 tablespoon of cooled tea per cloth
Continue for 3-5 days to see noticeable reduction in swelling

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling ailments such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The flavonoids and tannins present in this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections, thereby alleviating symptoms of the ailment. The bioactive constituents, including hyperforin and hypericin, possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and protect the affected eye area, promoting faster healing and recovery. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat eye swelling ailments include reduced inflammation, improved eye health, and prevention of future infections, making it a popular natural remedy among those seeking a holistic approach to eye care.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum leaves, or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of fresh leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce eye swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat eye swelling, but possible side effects include increased sensitivity to sunlight, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications that may affect blood thinning or blood pressure regulation. Precautions include starting with low doses, avoiding consumption during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being cautious when taking other herbal remedies to minimize potential interactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Hypericum perforatum tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Hypericum perforatum per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes before straining and drinking.
Continue for 3-5 days to alleviate eye swelling symptoms.
Consult a doctor before using Hypericum perforatum tea for extended periods.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling ailments such as edema and conjunctivitis. This herbal preparation helps to treat eye swelling by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which in turn helps to alleviate the symptoms of the ailment. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, work together to reduce inflammation and improve vascular function, which helps to treat the eye swelling ailment. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat eye swelling include reduced swelling and inflammation, improved vision, and enhanced overall eye health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the powder.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the liquid and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for eye swelling relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate eye swelling ailments, but possible side effects include nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its potential interaction with blood-thinning medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take precautions such as starting with low doses, avoiding consumption with other herbal preparations, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba tea in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, if any.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to reduce eye swelling.
Start with a dosage of 1/4 cup of tea and increase as needed.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling, also known as periorbital edema, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects and ability to soothe irritated tissues. By consuming Urtica dioica teas, individuals can benefit from its potential to reduce eye swelling and alleviate associated symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and stems or 2 teaspoons of dried Urtica dioica.
Combine the Urtica dioica with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid if using fresh Urtica dioica.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired. Stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce eye swelling. Consult a doctor before using as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of pharmacy & bioallied sciences", Urtica dioica teas may have anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic effects, which could potentially help in reducing eye swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used as a potential remedy for eye swelling, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and nausea in some individuals. When using Urtica dioica teas to treat this ailment, precautions should be taken to avoid over-consumption, as excessive intake may lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate underlying conditions, and it should also be avoided during pregnancy and breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup Urtica dioica tea for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and wring it out.
Apply the cooled cloth to the swollen eye for 5-10 minutes, 3 times a day.
Drink 1-2 cups of Urtica dioica tea daily to speed up healing.

preparations.herb_desc: Symphytum officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling ailments, such as conjunctivitis and eczema, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents, including allantoin, mucilage, and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling, promote healing, and provide relief from irritation. By acting as a protective barrier and reducing inflammation, Symphytum officinale teas help to treat eye swelling ailments by allowing the affected area to recover and regenerate. The benefits of this herbal preparation include reduced swelling, improved eye health, and a decrease in the risk of further complications, making it a popular natural remedy for eye-related issues.

Place the dried leaves in a cup and pour the boiling water over them.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Symphytum officinale teas can be used to treat eye swelling, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its potential allergic reactions and interactions with other medications. It is essential to take precautions when using Symphytum officinale teas, including avoiding high doses, mixing it with other herbal preparations, and being cautious if you have a history of allergies or are pregnant or breastfeeding.

Consume 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb per cup of tea

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been used to treat eye swelling ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which work together to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce swelling. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat eye swelling include reduced pain and discomfort, improved vision, and a lower risk of complications such as scarring or vision loss. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can cause allergic reactions, skin irritation, and dizziness in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Achillea millefolium teas safely, it's recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it if you have a history of allergic reactions or are pregnant/breastfeeding, as there is limited research on its effects on these groups.

Strain the tea after 5-7 minutes of steeping.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day.
Use the tea for 3-5 consecutive days for optimal results.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium myrtillus teas can be used to treat eye swelling ailments, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Vaccinium myrtillus teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, avoid mixing it with other medications or herbal preparations, and discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat eye swelling ailments, such as blepharitis, due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents like limonene, linalool, and rosmarinic acid, which help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause eye swelling. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Melissa officinalis teas can help to alleviate symptoms of eye swelling, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat eye swelling include reduced redness, swelling, and discomfort, allowing for a return to normal vision and daily activities.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, fresh or dried.
Measure 250 ml of boiling water into a heat-resistant cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be an effective treatment for eye swelling, but it may cause mild side effects such as allergic reactions, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high concentration of essential oils. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and be cautious when taking it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Arctium lappa teas have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling ailments, such as edema or conjunctivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the breakdown of excess fluids. The bioactive constituents of Arctium lappa teas, including polysaccharides, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, contribute to their therapeutic effects by modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress. The benefits of using Arctium lappa teas to treat eye swelling ailments include reduced swelling, improved vision, and accelerated recovery, making it a valuable natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Arctium lappa root and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and then reduce heat to medium.
Add the dried Arctium lappa root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the root. Let the tea cool down.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to the affected eye for 10-15 minutes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Arctium lappa teas can be used to treat eye swelling, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and increased heart rate in some individuals. Precautions when using this herbal preparation include being cautious with dosages, as excessive consumption may lead to stomach upset and diarrhea, and avoiding use during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential effects on the fetus or baby.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas have been traditionally used to treat eye swelling, also known as periorbital edema, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Equisetum arvense, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. By using Equisetum arvense teas, individuals can alleviate the symptoms of eye swelling and promote a faster recovery, resulting in improved visual comfort and overall well-being. The benefits of Equisetum arvense teas in treating eye swelling include reduced pain and swelling, improved eye mobility, and enhanced eye health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Equisetum arvense leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed to reduce eye swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used to treat eye swelling, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, and interactions with other herbal preparations that may increase the risk of bleeding. Precautions to take when using Equisetum arvense teas for this ailment include avoiding consumption if you have a history of bleeding disorders, and monitoring your body's response closely to adjust usage accordingly.
